skill.name: fda-consultant-specialist
skill.description: Provides FDA regulatory strategy support for medical device teams, including pathway planning, QSR readiness, HIPAA review, cybersecurity planning, and submission preparation.
skill.summary: Plan FDA medical device submissions, compliance reviews, and regulatory strategy.
skill.author: davila7

security_audit.summary: Static findings were evaluated in context and dismissed as false positives. The weak cryptography matches are ordinary regulatory and placeholder terms, and the backtick matches are Markdown code fences or inline file names. No prompt injection, command execution, data exfiltration, or malicious intent was found.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Pattern
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged weak cryptography at SKILL.md lines 3, 47-52, 57, 83, 115, assets/example_asset.txt line 10, and references/api_reference.md lines 15 and 26. These lines contain FDA, HIPAA, QSR, documentation, or placeholder terms, not cryptographic functions or security-sensitive code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines are natural language documentation and placeholders. I found no encryption calls, hashing functions, credential handling, or executable code at these locations.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Ruby Or Shell Backtick Pattern
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged backticks in SKILL.md lines 16, 41, 112, 135, and 229-244. These are Markdown code fences and inline file or directory names, not Ruby or shell command execution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The inspected content is Markdown formatting only. No Ruby file, shell script, command substitution, or instruction to execute these names was present.

content.value_statement: Medical device teams need clear regulatory planning before submission work begins. This skill helps organize FDA pathways, QSR controls, HIPAA safeguards, and cybersecurity documentation.
actual_capabilities: Maps medical device projects to FDA pathway options such as 510(k), PMA, and De Novo.
Outlines QSR compliance areas including design controls, document controls, CAPA, and management responsibility.
Structures HIPAA risk assessment work for devices that handle protected health information.
Summarizes FDA cybersecurity planning needs for connected devices and SaMD products.
Creates inspection readiness and warning letter response planning outlines.
Identifies regulatory strategy topics for submissions, Q-Sub meetings, and market access planning.
limitations: Does not replace legal advice, regulatory counsel, or a licensed compliance professional.
Does not submit materials to FDA or access FDA systems.
Does not validate device safety, clinical evidence, or test results.
Uses general guidance and needs current source review for final decisions.

use_cases.title: Choose a Submission Pathway
use_cases.description: Compare 510(k), PMA, and De Novo considerations for a device concept before formal planning.

use_cases.title: Prepare QSR Gap Reviews
use_cases.description: Organize design controls, document controls, CAPA, and management responsibility topics for internal assessment.

use_cases.title: Plan Connected Device Compliance
use_cases.description: Review HIPAA, cybersecurity, SaMD, and data protection questions for a connected medical device.

prompt_templates.title: Identify FDA Pathway Options
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner planning for a medical device concept.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this device concept and list likely FDA pathway options. Include key assumptions, missing information, and next regulatory questions.

prompt_templates.title: Build a QSR Gap Checklist
prompt_templates.scenario: Quality team preparing for internal review.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a QSR gap checklist for our device lifecycle. Cover design controls, document controls, CAPA, training, and management review.

prompt_templates.title: Assess HIPAA and Cybersecurity Needs
prompt_templates.scenario: Connected device team reviewing patient data flows.
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze these device data flows for HIPAA and FDA cybersecurity planning. Separate administrative, physical, technical, and post-market controls.

prompt_templates.title: Draft Submission Readiness Strategy
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced planning for a regulatory program.
prompt_templates.prompt: Develop a submission readiness strategy for this device. Include pathway rationale, evidence needs, Q-Sub topics, timeline risks, and inspection readiness.

output_examples.input: A wearable cardiac monitor records patient signals and sends alerts through a cloud dashboard.
output_examples.output: Likely output includes pathway questions, predicate research needs, SaMD considerations, cybersecurity documentation topics, and HIPAA safeguard areas.

output_examples.input: Our team needs a QSR readiness review before design verification begins.
output_examples.output: Likely output includes a structured checklist for design inputs, outputs, reviews, verification, validation, document controls, and CAPA readiness.

output_examples.input: We received FDA questions about software changes and risk controls.
output_examples.output: Likely output includes response planning topics, supporting evidence needs, traceability gaps, risk control documentation, and communication priorities.

best_practices: Provide device type, intended use, patient population, software role, and market history.
Confirm final regulatory positions against current FDA guidance and qualified counsel.
Separate planning outputs from approved quality records until reviewed by responsible owners.
anti_patterns: Do not treat generated guidance as an official FDA decision.
Do not include unnecessary PHI, secrets, or confidential patient details in prompts.
Do not use generic pathway advice without predicate, risk, and indication analysis.

faq.question: Can this skill determine my final FDA pathway?
faq.answer: No. It can structure pathway analysis, but final decisions need current evidence and qualified regulatory review.

faq.question: Does it handle HIPAA compliance planning?
faq.answer: Yes. It can organize HIPAA safeguards, data flow questions, and risk assessment topics for review.

faq.question: Can it write a complete 510(k) submission?
faq.answer: It can outline sections and readiness needs. It does not create validated submission evidence or submit to FDA.

faq.question: Is this useful for SaMD products?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes SaMD classification, software documentation, cybersecurity, and change control planning topics.

faq.question: Does it access external FDA databases?
faq.answer: No. The skill content does not include live database access or network integrations.

faq.question: What information should I provide?
faq.answer: Provide intended use, device function, risk profile, data flows, software role, and existing compliance documents.
